mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output, which drives higher corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of instances, or if the correlation between INDPRO changes and forward returns is not significantly positive (p>0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 5% signals an aggressive monetary tightening stance that increases the discount rate on equities, reducing present value of future cash flows and lowering forward returns.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS > 5%, the average 5-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p < 0.05) or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and rise in unemployment indicates a weakening demand-side environment that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 2% month-over-month and UNRATE rises > 0.1 percentage point over a 2-month period
failure_condition: If the joint condition is met but the median 60-day forward return is positive, or if the rank IC of the interaction factor is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is at or below 5%, a strong payroll gain signals an overheated labor market that will prompt the Fed to tighten monetary policy, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If during periods with UNRATE <= 5% and PAYEMS > 150k, the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and earnings higher, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If the correlation between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p>0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 200, inflation becomes a dominant concern, eroding real earnings and prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy, which reduces equity valuations over the short term.
failure_condition: If following CPIAUCSL > 200, the 5-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is rising and the Federal Reserve is simultaneously raising interest rates, it indicates that the economy is growing above potential and monetary policy is tightening to curb demand-pull inflation, a combination that depresses equity forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% and FEDFUNDS has increased by at least 25 basis points over the past 3 months
failure_condition: If the joint signal does not produce a negative median 20-day return in more than 60% of occurrences, or if the interaction term in a linear model is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle when the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, rising housing starts are interpreted as a sign of economic overheating that will prompt even tighter policy, thereby reducing equity valuations.
failure_condition: If during periods with FEDFUNDS > 4% and rising HOUST, the 20-day forward return is positive more often than negative, or if the average return is positive.
